title: Package registry and (import ...) special form
description: Need a central registry mapping package names to Register(scope) functions, and an (import strings) special form in eval.go that looks up the registry and loads the package into a namespace scope. This is the runtime hook that genpackage output plugs into.

title: Namespace scopes for package imports
description: Without namespacing, importing multiple packages pollutes the flat top-level scope and causes name collisions (e.g. strings.Contains vs bytes.Contains). Need sub-scopes so (import strings) puts symbols under a strings namespace, callable as (strings/contains ...).

title: Destructuring := form
description: The current := only binds a single symbol. To support Go-style error handling, need (:= (val err) expr) to unpack a two-element list returned by Go functions. This lets scripts branch on errors without exceptions.

title: Multi-value returns from Go functions
description: reflectResultToNode currently discards all return values after the first non-error one. For Go-style error handling in lisp scripts, functions returning (T, error) need to surface both values. The result should be a two-element list so lisp code can inspect both.

title: Runtime list/slice type for lisp
description: Functions like strings.Split return []string, which currently becomes an opaque GoValue the lisp cannot iterate or inspect. Need a native runtime list type that maps to/from Go slices, with basic operations (car, cdr, each, or similar).

title: Variadic function support in GoFunc
description: GoFunc currently rejects variadic functions with an error. This blocks binding large portions of stdlib (fmt.Println, fmt.Sprintf, strings.Join, etc). Need to support variadic Go functions by spreading lisp args into the variadic slice parameter.

title: genpackage: auto-generate Go->lisp bindings
description: Implement cmd/genpackage to take Go package import paths, use go/packages + go/types to enumerate exported functions, and emit a .go file with a Register(scope *lisp.Scope) function. Output plugs into the package registry so scripts can (import strings) etc.

title: Phase 4: GoFunc reflection adapter
description: Add GoFunc(fn any) Callable that wraps any Go func via reflection, auto-coercing atoms to/from Go primitives and GoValue/ObjectNode.

title: Phase 3: ObjectNode with reflection-based field/method access
description: Add ObjectNode{Fields map[string]Node}. WrapObject uses reflection to eagerly populate fields (GoValue) and methods (Callable). Add get builtin for field access via (get obj "Key").

title: Phase 2: GoValue wrapper and scope.Bind
description: Add GoValue{Value any} node wrapping arbitrary Go values. Add Wrap/Unwrap helpers and scope.Bind convenience method.

title: Phase 1: StringAtom and string lexing
description: Add STRING token type to the lexer for quoted string literals. Add StringAtom node type. Update builtins (=, println) to handle strings.
